pair.axis_differ: I2S substitution: CmpLogObserver + AFLppCmplogTracingStage vs none

hypothesis.mechanism: Cmp's I2S logs the constant operand of every integer comparison via sancov trace_const_cmp; mutator directly substitutes the logged constant into candidate input positions in one mutation step. Naive's random havoc must brute-force the constant byte-by-byte (1/256^N hit rate).
hypothesis.predicted_direction: A>B with monotone increasing cmp/naive ratio in MAGIC_BYTES

notes: Clean monotone increase in cmp/naive ratio across widths 1, 2, 4 (43x -> 135x -> 1097x).

Slight rebound at width=8 (1097x -> 588x): naive likely benefits from libafl's compile-time autotoken extraction, which splices the literal 0xDEADBEEFCAFEBABE directly into mutations regardless of width. For pure I2S effect demonstration, widths 1-4 are the cleanest.
Methodology converged on the right axis with disciplined protocol: artifact search + cross-target check + multi-candidate emission + falsification before building.

Real-target corroboration: bloaty/macho.cc:618 `if (magic == MH_MAGIC || magic == MH_MAGIC_64 || magic == FAT_CIGAM)`. 4-byte equality CMP against three literal constants (0xFEEDFACE, 0xFEEDFACF, 0xBEBAFECA). cmplog vs naive (ΔAUC=13535100, ΔFinal=620.33, n_A=3 n_B=3): cmplog resolved 3/3 trials (avg blocked 0.33h, hitcount 7917) while naive resolved 0/3 (blocked full 12h, hitcount 0). Side-B cmplog seeds all begin with `ce fa ed fe` (MH_MAGIC little-endian) at offset 0..3 — direct evidence the I2S dictionary substituted the constant. Side-A naive seeds carry WASM `\0asm` magic instead and never flip the branch. Replicates strict-wins on 2/2 I2S-labeled edges for bloaty (cmplog vs naive AND value_profile_cmplog vs value_profile, ΔAUC=12126600 on the second). This is the MAGIC_BYTES=4 dose point in the wild.

Real-target corroboration also confirms the disjunction-of-constants form does not change the mechanism: a chained `==` over 3 distinct 4-byte constants is still 1/(2^32) per-attempt for naive (3/2^32 ≈ 7e-10) and one-substitution-step for cmplog. The template's single-constant harness is sufficient to model this site.

Real-target corroboration #2 (bloaty/elf.cc:1279, branch 446, GetBuildId): `for (Elf64_Xword i = 1; i < elf.section_count(); i++)`. Surface CMP looks variable-vs-variable (loop counter vs parsed e_shnum), but the actual divergence-driver is upstream ELF-parser structural gates that I2S-substitution clears: 4-byte ELF magic 0x7F454C46, 1-byte e_ident class/data/version fields, 2-byte e_ehsize/e_phentsize/e_shentsize literal sizeof gates, and 2-byte e_shnum >= 2 to enter the loop body. cmplog vs naive (ΔAUC=13535100, ΔFinal=620.33, n_A=3 n_B=3): cmplog resolved 3/3 (avg blocked 0.00h, hitcount 9.35M) while naive resolved 0/3 (blocked 11.83h, hitcount 0). Both Side-A naive and Side-B cmplog seeds carry the 4-byte ELF magic at offset 0..3; the difference is downstream — Side-B cmplog seeds have well-formed structural fields populated by I2S substitution of parser sizeof-constants, yielding e_shnum >= 2 once the section-header table is parseable. Replicates strict-wins on 2/2 I2S-labeled edges. Mechanism is MAGIC_BYTES=4 (ELF magic) + a short chain of MAGIC_BYTES ∈ {1, 2} gates downstream — the per-CMP knob is identical to this template's MAGIC_BYTES axis. Considered and rejected for branch 446: a separate `i2s_loop_bound_reflection` axis where I2S would substitute the runtime-derived section_count value back into the input header field that produced it. Structurally possible but inconsistent with the observed cmplog profile (3/3 resolved at 0.00h avg blocked, 9.35M hitcount) which fits one-shot upstream gate substitutions, not a slow reflective loop.

Real-target corroboration #3 at width≈6 (string keyword): sqlite3/bld/sqlite3.c:103595, branch 1726, inside sqlite3ExprCodeTarget binary-op switch arm. Subject 13 cmplog vs naive (ΔAUC=9862800, ΔFinal=414.67): cmplog resolved 3/3, naive UNREACHED 3/3 trials. All 5 Side-B cmplog seeds begin with literal ASCII `SELECT` (53 45 4c 45 43 54) at offset 0..5 followed by expression tokens. Mutation lineage on the sample seed lists `WordAddMutator,BytesSwapMutator` — consistent with I2S having earlier substituted the 6-byte keyword constant logged from sqlite3's keyword-recognition CMP, after which standard havoc generated the trailing expression. Naive's failure is at the upstream 6-byte SELECT gate (1/256^6 ≈ 1/2.8e14 per attempt, unreachable in 12h × 600k execs/s ≈ 2.6e10 attempts), so the local branch is unreached, not blocked — same `naive impossible / cmp instant` regime the template predicts at width=8. Generalization: the template covers 'reachability gating by an upstream wide-constant CMP' as well as 'local wide-constant CMP'. A separate 'i2s_reachability_chain' template was considered and rejected — only ONE multi-byte literal at fixed offset (SELECT); trailing expression tokens are havoc-tractable. Effective chain length of dependent I2S substitutions is 1, not N. Strict-wins on 2/2 I2S-labeled edges for sqlite3.

Real-target corroboration #4 (n=10 trials, 2026-05-03, replicates corroboration #1 with stronger n): bloaty/macho.cc:152, branch 475, Mach-O entry-point magic check `if (magic == MH_MAGIC || magic == MH_MAGIC_64 || magic == MH_CIGAM || magic == MH_CIGAM_64)`. 4-way disjunction over 4-byte constants. cmplog 10/10 resolved, naive 0/10 (blocked). Replicates strict-wins on 2/2 I2S-labeled edges (cmplog>naive AND value_profile_cmplog>value_profile). Per-attempt naive hit rate 4/2^32 ≈ 9.3e-10 — same 1/(2^32) regime modulo a 4x linear factor dwarfed by the constant-width effect. Same MAGIC_BYTES=4 dose point as corroborations #1 and #2; disjunction width does not change the mechanism.

Real-target corroboration #5 at width=4 with massive downstream amplification (n=10 trials, 2026-05-03): lcms/cmsgamma.c:721, branch 26, EvalSegmentedFn for-condition `for (i = (int) g->nSegments - 1; i >= 0; --i)`. Subject 8 vpc vs vp (ΔAUC=23550300, ΔFinal=645.4, n_A=10 n_B=10): vpc resolved 10/10 (avg blocked 0.00h, hitcount 1,506,010), vp resolved 0/10 (blocked 11.80h, hitcount 0). Replicates strict-wins on 2/2 I2S-labeled edges for lcms (cmplog vs naive ΔAUC=17974800 AND vpc vs vp). Surface CMP at line 721 is a loop-counter comparison (variable-vs-variable), but the actual divergence-driver is upstream: the ICC profile header colorspace-signature 4-byte equality CMP. All 5 Side-B vpc seeds carry `4c 61 62 20 4c 61 62 20` ('Lab ' 'Lab ') at offsets 0x10–0x17 — the dataColorSpace and pcs fields of the ICC v4 header (cmsSigLabData = 0x4C616220, defined in lcms2.h). All 5 Side-A vp seeds carry `52 47 42 20 58 59 5a 20` ('RGB ' 'XYZ ') which routes the transform through a different code path that does not exercise EvalSegmentedFn at the per-pixel rate. Mutation lineage on Side-B includes DwordInterestingMutator/WordInterestingMutator plus standard havoc — consistent with cmplog's I2S substitution of the 4-byte signature constant logged from cmsSetColorSpace/cmsSetPCS validation CMPs. The 6-orders-of-magnitude hitcount asymmetry (1.5M vs 0) is NOT a new mechanism — it's the same downstream-amplification shape as bloaty/elf.cc:1279 (9.35M hitcount under ELF magic unlock): ONE I2S-substituted upstream gate enables a deeply-iterated downstream loop. Considered and rejected: a separate `i2s_loop_amplification` axis where dose-response would scale with loop-trip-count rather than constant-width — the gate that SELECTS whether the loop runs is still the 4-byte literal CMP; trip-count is data-determined, not fuzzer-controlled. Per-CMP knob is identical to MAGIC_BYTES at width=4. Effective chain length is 1. Strict-wins on 2/2 I2S-labeled edges for lcms.

Real-target corroboration #6 at width=4 with reachability gating via Mach-O magic (n=10 trials, 2026-05-03): bloaty/bloaty.cc:1750, branch 397, ScanAndRollupFile build-id presence check `if (!build_id.empty())`. Subject 4 vpc vs vp (ΔAUC=12738240, ΔFinal=511.6, p_AUC=0.0002, n_A=10 n_B=10): vpc resolved 10/10 (avg blocked 2.10h, hitcount 237), vp resolved 0/10 (blocked 12.00h, hitcount 0). Replicates strict-wins on 2/2 I2S-labeled edges for bloaty (cmplog vs naive ΔAUC=11951640 AND vpc vs vp ΔAUC=12738240). Surface CMP at line 1750 is a structural property check (`build_id.empty()`), variable-derived from upstream Mach-O LC_UUID parsing. The actual divergence-driver is the upstream 4-byte Mach-O magic equality CMP at bloaty.cc:152 (corroboration #4): all 5 Side-B vpc seeds begin with `ce fa ed fe` (MH_CIGAM = 0xFEEDFACE little-endian) at offset 0x00–0x03, while Side-A vp seeds carry `!<arch>` / `\0asm` / `\x7fELF` magics that route through code paths that don't populate build_id at this site. Mutation lineage on Side-B (BytesDeleteMutator, BytesExpandMutator, TokenInsert) is consistent with cmplog having earlier substituted the 4-byte MH_CIGAM constant from the macho.cc:152 disjunction CMP, after which standard havoc evolves the load-command sequence to include LC_UUID. This is a direct overlap with corroboration #4 (same 4-byte magic, same target, same fuzzer pair) plus reachability-gating identical to corroboration #2 (build_id-empty check is reachability-gated like elf.cc:1279's loop-bound check). The local CMP itself is NOT I2S-substitutable (no literal operand) — it merely reports whether the Mach-O parser succeeded. Per-CMP knob is MAGIC_BYTES=4. Effective chain length of dependent I2S substitutions is 1 (the macho-magic gate). Considered and rejected: a separate `i2s_structural_property_gate` template where the predictor would be 'fraction-of-blockers-downstream-of-an-I2S-substitutable-magic' — this is a target-side observation about how far reachability propagates from one unlocked gate, not a per-CMP fuzzer-mechanism axis. The dose-response would still be MAGIC_BYTES at the gate, identical to this template's existing prediction. Strict-wins on 2/2 I2S-labeled edges for bloaty.

Real-target corroboration #7 at width=4 (string-equality form, WASM custom-section name; n=10 trials, 2026-05-03): bloaty/webassembly.cc:505, branch 505, `if (section.name == "name") {`. Subject value_profile_cmplog vs value_profile (axis_differ = I2S added on top of vp): vpc resolves, vp blocks. Replicates strict-wins on 2/2 I2S-labeled edges for bloaty/webassembly (cmplog vs naive AND vpc vs vp). Surface CMP is a `std::string operator==` against the 4-byte ASCII literal `"name"` (6e 61 6d 65) — same per-CMP shape as MAGIC_BYTES=4 (at the std::string layer the comparator reduces to memcmp(p, "name", 4) once the length-equality precheck passes; sancov logs the 4-byte literal operand). vp-without-I2S faces 1/256^4 ≈ 1/4.3e9 random search at this site; adding I2S (= vpc) substitutes the literal in one step. To REACH this comparison the parser also requires a short upstream chain of I2S-substitutable literal gates that vpc clears in the same way: (a) WASM module magic 4-byte `\0asm` (00 61 73 6d) at file offset 0..3, (b) version field 4-byte `\1\0\0\0` at offset 4..7, (c) section-id byte = 0x00 (custom-section indicator, 1-byte literal CMP), (d) ULEB128 section length — length-encoding, not a literal CMP, havoc-tractable once the preceding literals are pinned. Effective chain length of dependent I2S substitutions is 3 fixed-offset literals (4+4+1 = 9 constraint bytes upstream) plus the local 4-byte string-equality — same cascade shape as the macho/elf corroborations (#1, #2, #4) where an outer magic gates an inner field check. Per-CMP knob is identical to MAGIC_BYTES at width=4. Considered and rejected: a separate `i2s_string_equality` axis distinct from numeric MAGIC_BYTES — std::string ==, after the length precheck, lowers to a fixed-width memcmp against a constant byte sequence and is logged by sancov trace_const_cmp the same way numeric equality is; the substitution mechanism is identical and the dose-response point at width=4 is already covered by this template's existing data.

Real-target corroboration #8 at width≈6 (sqlite3 second within-target replication of the SELECT-keyword gate; n=10 trials, 2026-05-03): sqlite3/bld/sqlite3.c:28731, branch 1311, `if( prefix ){` inside sqlite3VXPrintf's etFLOAT/etEXP/etGENERIC numeric formatting arm. Subject 13 cmplog vs naive (ΔAUC=24990300, ΔFinal=536.0, p_final=0.0173, n_A=10 n_B=10): cmplog resolved 9/10 (avg blocked 0.50h, hitcount 64), naive resolved 0/10 (blocked 4/10, unreached 6/10, hitcount 0). Replicates strict-wins on 2/2 I2S-labeled edges for sqlite3. All 5 Side-B cmplog seeds begin with literal ASCII `SELECT` (53 45 4c 45 43 54) at offset 0..5; payload is `SELECT-111...;` — leading minus produces a negative numeric literal whose %g/%e/%f formatting code-path sets prefix='-'. Side-A naive seeds (the 4 reaching trials) ALSO carry `SELECT` at offset 0..5 — lineage shows naive eventually stumbled onto the keyword via BytesInsertMutator/WordAddMutator after thousands of seconds (discovered_at 337s–38031s) but produced non-negative numerics so prefix=0. Mechanism is identical to corroboration #3 (sqlite3 br 1726 in sqlite3ExprCodeTarget): the SAME upstream 6-byte SELECT keyword CMP gates reachability of multiple deeply-nested downstream branches in different sqlite3 functions. cmplog substitutes SELECT in one I2S step from sqlite3's keyword-recognition CMP; naive must brute-force 1/256^6 ≈ 1/2.8e14 per attempt and only reaches the branch in 4/10 trials over 12h. The local 1-byte sign-prefix condition (prefix='-' iff numeric is negative) is havoc-tractable once SELECT unlocks reachability — NOT the divergence-driver. Considered and rejected: forking into a separate template for the local sign-prefix condition (effective MAGIC_BYTES=1, but width=1 is already a dose point in this template's sweep). Considered and rejected: an `i2s_within_target_chain_amplification` template where one upstream keyword unlocks N downstream branches — within-target replication count is an OBSERVATION about how a single I2S substitution amplifies, not a fuzzer-controllable knob, so dose-response would scale with target structure not parameter. Per-CMP knob is identical to MAGIC_BYTES≈6. Within-target second-replication (br 1311 distinct from br 1726) strengthens the claim that the SELECT-gate effect is robust across distinct downstream branches in sqlite3.

Real-target corroboration #9 at width=8 (sentinel-value form, generalizes 'magic' → 'sentinel literal'; n=10 trials, 2026-05-03): bloaty/bloaty.cc:1315, branch 393, `if (vmsize == RangeMap::kUnknownSize || filesize == RangeMap::kUnknownSize)` inside RangeSink::AddRange. RangeMap::kUnknownSize is the 64-bit literal UINT64_MAX (0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF) used as a sentinel for 'size unknown'. Subject 4 vpc vs vp (ΔAUC=12738240, ΔFinal=511.6, p_AUC=0.0002, p_final=0.0002, n_A=10 n_B=10): vpc resolved 8/10 (avg blocked 6.15h, hitcount 16), vp resolved 0/10 (blocked 11.15h, hitcount 0). Replicates strict-wins on 2/2 I2S-labeled edges for bloaty (cmplog vs naive AND vpc vs vp). The local CMP at line 1315 is a 64-bit equality against a literal sentinel — same per-CMP shape as MAGIC_BYTES=8: sancov logs the literal kUnknownSize operand, cmplog substitutes it directly into candidate input positions in one mutation step; naive faces 1/(2^64) random search to land vmsize on UINT64_MAX. Sancov's trace_const_cmp does not distinguish 'magic' from 'sentinel' — both are integer literals at equality CMPs and both lower to the identical I2S substitution path. Side-A vs Side-B byte diff confirms upstream gating is identical to corroborations #4 and #6: all 5 Side-B vpc seeds begin with `ce fa ed fe` (MH_CIGAM = 0xFEEDFACE little-endian Mach-O magic) at offset 0x00–0x03 with embedded ELF as secondary structure later in the file; all 5 Side-A vp seeds carry `7f 45 4c 46` (ELF magic) at offset 0x00–0x03 and route through bloaty's elf.cc parser which never produces an unknown-size segment at this site. The reachability-gating chain to this CMP is the 4-byte Mach-O magic (corroboration #4) plus Mach-O load-command parsing populating one of the LC_SEGMENT_64 or LC_SEGMENT entries with vmsize=UINT64_MAX (or filesize=UINT64_MAX). Side-B mutation lineage includes ByteInterestingMutator/DwordInterestingMutator/QwordAddMutator — consistent with cmplog having earlier substituted the 4-byte MH_CIGAM constant from macho.cc:152 and then an 8-byte sentinel constant logged from the AddRange CMP itself. Effective chain length of dependent I2S substitutions is 2 (4-byte Mach-O magic gate + 8-byte sentinel at this CMP). Per-CMP knob is identical to MAGIC_BYTES at width=8. Considered and rejected: a separate `i2s_sentinel_value` template that distinguishes 'sentinel-as-error-marker' from 'magic-as-format-identifier'. The semantic role of the constant (file-format identifier vs error-state sentinel) does not change the fuzzer mechanism — sancov logs both as integer literals at trace_const_cmp; the I2S dictionary substitution path is byte-identical. Dose-response is identical to MAGIC_BYTES=8 (this corroboration corroborates the data point in this template's sweep where cmp/naive ratio at width=8 was 588x). The 'sentinel' framing is a target-side semantic observation, not a per-CMP fuzzer-mechanism axis. Considered and rejected: an `i2s_two_step_chain` axis where dose-response would scale with chain length — chain length is a structural property of the target's parser, not a fuzzer-controllable knob; per-CMP width remains the operative parameter. Strict-wins on 2/2 I2S-labeled edges for bloaty (br 393; replicates corroborations #1, #4, #6 within bloaty as the third within-target Mach-O reachability cascade).

Real-target corroboration #10 at width=4 (harness-level pre-check form, ICC colorspace signature; n=10 trials, 2026-05-03): lcms/cms_transform_fuzzer.cc:32, branch 358, `if (srcCS == cmsSigLabData) {` inside the libFuzzer harness wrapper itself, before lcms target code is entered. cmsSigLabData = 0x4C616220 ('Lab ' as 4-byte ASCII signature, defined in lcms2.h as one of the ICC colorspace signature constants). Subject 8 vpc vs vp, group I2S wins (strict 7/7 admissibility): loser_blocked=10/10, winner_resolved=10/10, prob_diff=1.0, hit_diff=106.3, n_edges=2 (replicates strict-wins on both cmplog vs naive AND vpc vs vp I2S-labeled edges for lcms). The local CMP is a 4-byte unsigned equality against a literal — same per-CMP shape as MAGIC_BYTES=4: sancov's trace_const_cmp logs the literal cmsSigLabData operand at compile time, cmplog substitutes it directly into candidate input positions in one mutation step; vp-without-I2S faces 1/256^4 ≈ 1/4.3e9 random search to land srcCS on 0x4C616220. This branch is the UPSTREAM ENTRY-GATE for corroboration #5 (lcms/cmsgamma.c:721, EvalSegmentedFn, 1.5M-vs-0 hitcount asymmetry under 'Lab ' unlock): the 'Lab ' bytes that drive the deeply-iterated downstream loop come THROUGH this harness pre-check first. Two distinct DB branches (358 here, 26 in corroboration #5) both gated by the same 4-byte cmsSigLabData literal — the harness CMP is the magic-gate, the cmsgamma loop is the downstream amplification. Considered and rejected: a separate `i2s_harness_level_pre_check` template arguing harness-authored CMPs are a distinct fuzzer-mechanism unit from target-internal CMPs. Falsification: sancov's trace_const_cmp instrumentation is inserted by the compiler at every `cmp r, imm` operation regardless of which translation unit the source came from. The harness file (cms_transform_fuzzer.cc) is compiled with the SAME -fsanitize-coverage=trace-cmp instrumentation flags as the target lcms code; cmplog's I2S dictionary observes the literal operand IDENTICALLY. The 'harness vs target' distinction is a human source-organization property, not a fuzzer-observable property — at the sancov-instrumentation layer the two are byte-identical. This is confirmed by the cross-call observation that branch 358 (harness CMP) and branch 26 (target-internal CMP, corroboration #5) BOTH show strict-wins on the same vpc-vs-vp edge with the same 4-byte 'Lab ' literal driving the substitution. Considered and rejected: an `i2s_chain_length` template where dose-response would scale with the number of dependent literal CMPs in series — already explicitly rejected in corroborations #2, #5, #6, #7, #9 of this same template. Each I2S substitution is independent and one-shot per CMP; chain length is target structure, not fuzzer-controllable. Per-CMP knob is identical to MAGIC_BYTES at width=4. Effective chain length of dependent I2S substitutions for branch 358 is 1 (the harness pre-check is itself the entry literal CMP — no upstream literal gating in the harness). Strict-wins on 2/2 I2S-labeled edges for lcms; this is the second within-target replication of the cmsSigLabData='Lab ' gate (358 distinct from 26), strengthening the claim that the same 4-byte ICC signature literal drives multiple distinct downstream branches in lcms via a single I2S substitution. Within-target second-replication parallels corroboration #8's role within sqlite3 (br 1311 distinct from br 1726, both gated by the SELECT keyword).

Real-target corroboration #11 at width=4 (variable-vs-variable downstream branch reachability-gated by upstream ELF/Mach-O magic, routing-divergence form; n=10 trials, 2026-05-03): bloaty/range_map.cc:315, branch 497, RangeMap::CoversRange while-loop check `} else if (it == mappings_.end() || !EntryContains(it, addr)) {`. Subject 4 vpc vs vp (ΔAUC=12738240, ΔFinal=511.6, p_AUC=0.0002, p_final=0.0002, n_A=10 n_B=10): vpc resolved 10/10 (avg blocked 3.00h, hitcount 25), vp resolved 2/10 (blocked 8/10, avg blocked 10.25h, hitcount 2). Replicates strict-wins on 2/2 I2S-labeled edges for bloaty (cmplog vs naive AND vpc vs vp). The local CMP at line 315 is purely variable-vs-variable (iterator end-check + range-membership predicate) — sancov logs NO literal at this site, so I2S substitution cannot help directly here. The divergence-driver is upstream: all 4 Side-B vpc seeds carry ELFCLASS32 routing (`7f 45 4c 46 01 01 07 01` at offsets 0x00–0x07: ELF magic + EI_CLASS=01 + EI_DATA=01 + EI_VERSION=07 + EI_OSABI=01) plus a populated non-zero e_entry (`fb ff 1c 34 0f 02 00 00` at offset 0x10–0x17), e_phoff (`01 00 00 00 80 00 00 00` at offset 0x18–0x1F), e_shoff (`08 00 00 00 64 02 00 00` at offset 0x20–0x27), and e_phnum/e_shnum-bearing structural fields. All 5 Side-A vp seeds carry ELFCLASS64 routing (`7f 45 4c 46 02 01 01 00` at offset 0x00–0x07) with e_entry=0 and zeroed program/section header pointers, which routes bloaty's ElfFile<64> parser through code paths that either add no ranges or add contiguously-covering ranges (so CoversRange's `EntryContains` always succeeds and the false branch is taken every time). The reachability-gating chain to this CMP is: (a) 4-byte ELF magic at file offset 0x00–0x03 (literal-equality CMP — cmplog I2S substitutes in one step), (b) 1-byte EI_CLASS at offset 0x04 (literal-equality CMP against ELFCLASS32=0x01 OR ELFCLASS64=0x02 — width-1 I2S gate), (c) downstream sizeof-equality CMPs in elf.cc header-validation against e_ehsize/e_phentsize/e_shentsize literals (width-2 I2S gates, identical to corroboration #2), and (d) loop iteration over the program/section headers populating RangeMap via RangeSink::AddRange with addresses derived from p_vaddr/p_filesz fields that produce sparse mappings (creating gaps where mappings_.end() is reachable). Side-B mutation lineage (WordInterestingMutator, QwordAddMutator, ByteRandMutator, CrossoverInsertMutator) is consistent with cmplog having earlier substituted the 4-byte ELF magic and 1-byte EI_CLASS constants from elf.cc parser CMPs, after which standard havoc evolved the program/section header table to have non-overlapping ranges. Per-CMP knob is MAGIC_BYTES=4 at the ELF magic gate; effective chain length of dependent I2S substitutions is 2 (4-byte ELF magic + 1-byte EI_CLASS routing-byte). Considered and rejected: a separate `i2s_routing_byte_class_dispatch` template where dose-response would scale with the number of distinct EI_CLASS values the parser dispatches on (32 vs 64 vs invalid). Class-dispatch byte is just MAGIC_BYTES=1 at sancov's trace_const_cmp1; the dispatch fan-out is a target-side parser property, not a fuzzer-controllable knob — width=1 is already a dose point in this template's sweep (cmp/naive ratio 43x). Considered and rejected: a separate `i2s_range_map_gap_reachability` template framed around 'whether mappings_ has gaps' as the program-feature axis. Gap structure is data-determined by what addresses the ELF parser pulls from p_vaddr/p_filesz fields, not a fuzzer-controlled parameter; the local CMP at line 315 is variable-vs-variable and contains no literal that I2S can substitute. The fuzzer-mechanism axis remains the upstream literal-magic CMP that gates whether the parser even runs — identical shape to corroborations #2 (elf.cc:1279 section_count loop), #5 (lcms cmsgamma.c:721 EvalSegmentedFn), #6 (bloaty.cc:1750 build_id.empty()), and #9 (bloaty.cc:1315 kUnknownSize sentinel) where the local CMP is variable-vs-variable but reachability is gated by an upstream literal-magic CMP. Strict-wins on 2/2 I2S-labeled edges for bloaty (br 497; replicates corroborations #1, #4, #6, #9 within bloaty as the fourth within-target ELF/Mach-O reachability cascade — first one through the ELFCLASS32 routing branch specifically, complementing #2 which exercised the ELFCLASS64 branch). Distinct from corroboration #10 (lcms cms_transform_fuzzer.cc:32 cmsSigLabData harness pre-check) which is upstream entry-gating; this corroboration is downstream variable-vs-variable reachability-gating, where the local CMP carries no literal but reachability into the RangeMap query path is unlocked by the upstream ELF magic + EI_CLASS routing-byte chain.

Real-target corroboration #12 at width=4 (switch-case dispatch form, FOURCC signature; n=10 trials, 2026-05-03): lcms/cmscnvrt.c:429, branch 1, `case cmsSigXYZData:` inside DefaultICCintents's outer `switch (InPCS)` over ICC color-space signatures.
